card_description: A sustaining divine presence preserving reality against entropy; named for the substance in PKD's 1969 novel.
full_description:
A sustaining divine presence preserving reality against entropy; named for the substance in PKD's 1969 novel.

**Category:** Top Term

**Frequency:** 1527 mentions. First appears 1975-02-27.

**From the Exegesis:**
> Dear Claudia,

I just had a bizarre thought. The decay backward of forms which Joe Chip sees...it is not decay. This is not retrogression. It is the retrograde axis.

We already pointed this axis out --the one he sees in UBIK-- as the orthogonal one. Now I say, its retrograde motion, expressed in...
> Last edit over 5 years ago by Toso
2
Indexed
2
never realized the analog between my seeing Rome, then the Hellenistic World, then Attika, then Crete, and what Joe et al in UBIK saw. Form reversion. Along the orthogonal axis. Wrong. Orthogonal (i.e the Forms) yes; reversion, no. Now, when I saw wh...
> Last edit over 5 years ago by Toso
3
Complete
3
Tessa's name for me (without her knowing all this, supra) is Rowie Rog. A combination of sounds which pierce to my soul, for evident reasons. Rog Phillips, by the way, was a friend of Tony Boucher, also dead...Ich Bin die liethe etc. Preface to UBIK...

**Key segment contexts:**
- (February 27, 1975) The author writes to Claudia, interpreting the "decay backward of forms" seen by the character Joe Chip in the novel *UBIK* as movement along the "orthogonal" axis of time. He claims this retrograd...
- (February 27, 1975) Dick explores the "Fallen" state of humanity through the metaphor of a fairy child in the mortal world (*The Red Feather*). He concludes that orthogonal time leads back to a childhood state where t...
- (circa 1975-1976) Dick explores the subjective vs. objective nature of his 1974 experience, concluding that the Parousia has occurred through a "retrograde" system of time. He compares reality to a film or LP record...

**Related terms:** Anamnesis (related), Because (related), Christ (related), Christianity (related), Complete (related), Dionysos (related), Fall (related)

evidence.text:
Dear Claudia,

I just had a bizarre thought. The decay backward of forms which Joe Chip sees...it is not decay. This is not retrogression. It is the retrograde axis.

We already pointed this axis out --the one he sees in UBIK-- as the orthogonal one. Now I say, its retrograde motion, expressed in the novel, is its proper development, its own forward.

This "decay" is the same direction the Holy Spirit (or whatever you wish to deem it) was moving. Backward vis-a-vis our own time. So Joe Chip isn'

never realized the analog between my seeing Rome, then the Hellenistic World, then Attika, then Crete, and what Joe et al in UBIK saw. Form reversion. Along the orthogonal axis. Wrong. Orthogonal (i.e the Forms) yes; reversion, no. Now, when I saw what I saw, I had "died" in an actual sense; like those in UBIK I came to the end point of my lineal time-line. But I wasn't depleted; this "decay backward" to Rome which I saw wasn't an entropic process,
